task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless execution intent-store boundary that names the planned durable hosted intent store, transition rules, idempotency requirements, retention rules, and safeguards before any queue intent persistence exists. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, Redis/Postgres connections, durable intent persistence, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.

Intent-store boundary must keep persistence, live/provider/worker/result-write paths denied until hosted auth, vault-handle, quota-reservation, lease-store, retry scheduling, and idempotent commit storage exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this boundary is a planned durable store contract and not real queue intent persistence.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing execution queue, intent preview, worker lease boundary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse existing Pro queue boundary payloads, API route handlers, web fetchJson helper, and preview-only boundary panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic execution intent-store boundary payload that names future durable transition, idempotency, and retention rules while reporting persistence disabled.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned by this intent-store boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue tests prove the intent-store boundary keeps persistence disabled and lists transition, idempotency, retention, and safeguard rules.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed execution intent-store boundary payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the intent-store boundary panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real durable queue persistence, provider execution, credential access, quota mutation, or auth enforcement.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless execution intent-store boundary endpoint.
Pro web shell renders whether durable hosted intent persistence is connected and which transition/idempotency/retention rules are still planned.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local intent-store boundary contract. It prepares durable transition and idempotency vocabulary without enabling provider calls, credential reads, quota reservations, queue persistence, worker execution, Redis/Postgres connections, or result writes.
